title: Commentary on Membership
content:
(Members data presented are as of December 31, 2026)
Campus reported 44 current members as of December 31, 2026. Membership levels remained relatively stable at 0% from the previous year end and increased by 2% on a quarter-over-quarter basis. Potential membership totaled 156 remaining unchanged, providing a sizeable base for future membership development initiatives.

Operational efficiency metrics remain variable. Members per full-time employee have shown variability, indicating that staffing levels show some misalignment with current service demand and operational requirements. This balance supports member service delivery while adjusting operating efficiency.

Shares per member increased during the period, consistent with a strengthening of deposit balances and improved funding stability. This trend underscores our commitment to maintaining strong capitalization for members as we navigate evolving market conditions.

Overall, membership trends remain stable despite modest gains on a quarterly basis. The institution retains meaningful opportunity to expand within its existing potential membership base. Continued focus on member engagement, product penetration, and service accessibility will remain important to supporting sustainable membership growth and long-term balance-sheet development.
